skill.name: paid-ads
skill.description: When the user wants help with paid advertising campaigns on Google Ads, Meta (Facebook/Instagram), LinkedIn, Twitter/X, or other ad platforms. Also use when the user mentions 'PPC,' 'paid media,' '...
skill.summary: Expert guidance for creating, optimizing, and scaling paid advertising campaigns across Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and TikTok.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a comprehensive paid advertising guide. All 23 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner misidentified markdown code blocks as shell commands, marketing terminology ('targeting', 'bidding') as system reconnaissance, and YAML frontmatter as cryptographic algorithms. This is a pure documentation/marketing guide with no executable code, network requests, or security risks.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Blocks Misidentified as Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged lines 110-179 as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are markdown code fences showing account structure diagrams and ad copy frameworks - standard documentation formatting, not executable code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These are clearly markdown code blocks using backticks for formatting. No shell or Ruby execution occurs - the skill is a static documentation guide.

dangerous_patterns.title: Marketing Terminology Misidentified as System Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner flagged terms like 'targeting', 'bidding', 'campaigns' as system reconnaissance. These are standard paid advertising terms, not security threats.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This skill teaches paid advertising best practices. 'Audience targeting', 'bid strategies', and 'campaign structure' are standard marketing terms, not security vulnerabilities.

dangerous_patterns.title: YAML Frontmatter Misidentified as Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner flagged YAML frontmatter 'risk: unknown' as a cryptographic issue. This is metadata, not code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The YAML frontmatter 'risk: unknown' is skill metadata. The other flagged lines contain text like 'Desired future state' - clearly not cryptographic code.

content.user_title: Create and Optimize Paid Ad Campaigns
content.value_statement: This skill provides expert guidance for creating, managing, and scaling paid advertising campaigns across major platforms including Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and TikTok.
actual_capabilities: Guide campaign strategy and platform selection based on business goals
Provide ad copy frameworks (PAS, BAB, Social Proof) for high-converting ads
Recommend audience targeting strategies for each platform
Explain campaign structure best practices and naming conventions
Detail creative best practices for images and video ads
Outline optimization strategies and key metrics by campaign objective
limitations: Does not provide access to ad platform accounts or APIs
Cannot create actual ad campaigns or make direct platform changes
Does not include real-time bidding or automated optimization
Does not provide specific financial projections or budget recommendations

use_cases.title: New Campaign Planning
use_cases.description: A marketing manager launching their first paid advertising campaign can use this skill to select the right platform, structure their campaigns correctly, and follow best practices from the start.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing managers and small business owners new to paid advertising

use_cases.title: Multi-Platform Strategy
use_cases.description: A digital marketer managing campaigns across Google, Meta, and LinkedIn can use this skill to understand platform-specific nuances and optimize each channel effectively.
use_cases.target_user: Digital marketers handling multi-platform ad campaigns

use_cases.title: Campaign Optimization
use_cases.description: An advertising specialist looking to improve underperforming campaigns can use this skill to diagnose issues and apply proven optimization techniques.
use_cases.target_user: Advertising specialists seeking to improve campaign performance

prompt_templates.title: New Campaign Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a paid advertising campaign for [product/service]. I want to [goal: drive sales/leads/traffic]. My monthly budget is [amount]. Which platform should I use and how should I structure my campaign?
prompt_templates.scenario: When starting a new paid advertising initiative and needing platform selection and structure guidance

prompt_templates.title: Ad Copy Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write ad copy for a [platform] campaign promoting [product]. The target audience is [description]. Use a Problem-Agitate-Solution framework and include a strong call to action.
prompt_templates.scenario: When needing to create compelling ad copy for a specific platform and audience

prompt_templates.title: Audience Targeting
prompt_templates.prompt: What audience targeting strategies should I use for [platform] when advertising [product] to [target audience description]? Include both core audience and custom audience options.
prompt_templates.scenario: When developing audience targeting strategy for a specific platform

prompt_templates.title: Campaign Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: My [platform] campaign has [issue: high CPA/low CTR/high CPM]. The current metrics are [data]. What optimization steps should I take to improve performance?
prompt_templates.scenario: When diagnosing and fixing underperforming campaign metrics

output_examples.input: Help me set up a Google Ads campaign for a B2B SaaS product with a $5000 monthly budget. Goal is to drive demo signups.
output_examples.output:
Here's a recommended structure for your B2B SaaS Google Ads campaign:

**Campaign 1: Brand Protection**
- Objective: Protect branded search terms
- Budget: 10% ($500)

**Campaign 2: Search - Product**
- Objective: Conversions
- Keywords: [product category] software, [competitor] alternative
- Budget: 50% ($2,500)

**Campaign 3: Display - Remarketing**
- Objective: Brand awareness and retargeting
- Audience: Website visitors
- Budget: 20% ($1,000)

**Campaign 4: YouTube - Consideration**
- Objective: Video views and consideration
- Budget: 20% ($1,000)

output_examples.input: Write ad copy for a Meta Ads campaign promoting a project management tool to small business owners.
output_examples.output:
**Primary Text (Problem-Agitate-Solution):**

Still using spreadsheets to manage your projects?

While you're manually updating cells and hoping nothing falls through the cracks, your competitors are closing deals faster.

[Product Name] gives small teams a centralized platform to track tasks, collaborate in real-time, and hit every deadline.

Start your free trial →

**Headline Options:**
- Project Management That Teams Actually Use
- Stop Managing Projects in Spreadsheets
- The Simple Tool for Growing Teams

**CTA:** Start Free Trial

best_practices: Start with clear campaign objectives and measurable KPIs before launching
Implement conversion tracking before spending any budget
Test one variable at a time to isolate what improves performance
Allow campaigns 2-4 weeks in learning phase before major optimizations
anti_patterns: Launching campaigns without proper conversion tracking in place
Trying to optimize for multiple metrics simultaneously
Making large budget changes that disrupt algorithmic learning
Neglecting mobile experience in creative and landing page design

faq.question: Which platform should I start with for paid advertising?
faq.answer: Start with the platform where your audience spends time and where you have existing data. Google Ads is best for high-intent search traffic. Meta is best for visual products and demand generation. LinkedIn is best for B2B targeting.

faq.question: How much budget do I need to run effective campaigns?
faq.answer: Minimum budgets vary by platform. Google Ads typically needs $1,000+/month minimum for meaningful data. Meta can work with $500+/month. LinkedIn requires higher budgets due to higher CPCs, typically $2,000+/month.

faq.question: How long does it take to see results from paid ads?
faq.answer: Plan for 2-4 weeks of learning phase before algorithms optimize. Significant results typically appear after 4-8 weeks. For conversion campaigns, you need 50+ conversions per variation for statistical significance.

faq.question: What metrics should I track for conversion campaigns?
faq.answer: Track CPA (cost per acquisition), ROAS (return on ad spend), conversion rate, and cost per click. Compare these against your target CPA/ROAS to determine campaign health.

faq.question: How often should I refresh my ad creative?
faq.answer: Monitor frequency metrics. Refresh creative when frequency exceeds 3-4x per week for the same audience, or when CTR drops below 1% on Google or 0.5% on Meta.

faq.question: Can I manage multiple platforms at once?
faq.answer: Yes, but start with one platform until you have stable performance data. Managing multiple platforms requires more budget to gather enough data for optimization. Focus on dominating one platform before expanding.
